mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 65% of rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and corporate profit forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand growth that boosts corporate revenue projections, generating positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a statistically significant positive mean return over 60 trading days with p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during post-shock recovery phases trigger short-term market panic and liquidity drains, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat during industrial production contractions within post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and consumer purchasing power, supporting corporate earnings growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between payroll growth and 20-day forward returns drops below 0.05 or turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.3 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing GDP growth and accelerating retail sales signals a consumption-led economic expansion that outperforms broader output metrics, driving positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDP growth and retail sales growth yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample return regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real interest rates during a monetary tightening cycle increase the cost of capital and reduce present values of growth stocks, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or flat when nominal rates rise faster than inflation during documented tightening cycles.
